title: polypodium phyllitidis var. repens
intro: Polypodium phyllitidis var. repens, also known as Creeping Polypody, is a type of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of certain diseases. Creeping Polypody can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being and promote natural healing.
